figures.caption: Figure 1: Overview of EFFIPAIR. Given a coding task, the LLM samples N candidate programs. Candidates are checked for correctness and profiled for efficiency, then stored in a candidate pool. EFFIPAIR selects a pair consisting of an efficient reference program p+ and a similar but less efficient candidate p−, summarizes their relative execution differences into compact Relative Contrastive Feedback (RCF), and uses this signal to refine the program. The refined program is added back to the pool for subsequent rounds.
Table 2: Cross-benchmark comparison with prior methods.

Table 4: Token usage by model and refinement setting. Total tokens are the sum of prompt and completion tokens. Full-profile variants incur substantially higher prompt costs than summarized-profile and paired methods.
Figure 4: DPS, DPSnorm, and Pass@1 for various similarity thresholds.
Figure 3: DPS, DPSnorm, and Pass@1 for various embedding weights.
Figure 2: Iterative refinement behavior of EFFIPAIR on EvalPerf across evaluated models.

Table 1: Model-level comparison of baseline generation, paired prompting without profiling, single-candidate with summarized profiling, and EFFIPAIR. Green annotations in the EFFIPAIR row indicate improvements relative to the corresponding Solo baseline.
